summary: Pipeline works end-to-end in ~2 minutes. All issues resolved.

issue.title: gpt-5.4-pro Responses API — RESOLVED
issue.description: The Azure gpt-5.4-pro model only supports the Responses API. Previously, the pipeline used AzureChatOpenAI (Chat Completions wrapper) with reasoning_effort='medium', causing 10+ minute generation times. Additionally, the validation runner had bugs that always triggered the slow repair loop.
root_cause_analysis: agent/llm.py used AzureChatOpenAI which targets Chat Completions API, not the Responses API — forced reasoning_effort='medium' and temperature=1 workarounds
Plan prompt was too open-ended with no concrete JSON example, triggering deep extended reasoning
Clarify node made an unnecessary LLM call for short prompts, doubling latency
validation/runner.py passed string path instead of ifcopenshell.file object to ifctester
ifctester.reporter.Json had a bug (UnboundLocalError on cardinality)
hk_code.ids used IFCLABEL datatype for IsExternal instead of IFCBOOLEAN
custom.ids was empty and caused ifctester to reject it, failing the whole validation
Validation failures triggered repair node which made another slow LLM call that timed out
fixes_applied: Replaced AzureChatOpenAI with custom AzureResponsesChatModel that directly POSTs to the Responses API (adopted from v1 reference)
Removed reasoning_effort, temperature=1, max_tokens — not needed with direct Responses API calls
Rewrote plan.txt with concrete JSON example matching BuildingPlan schema exactly — reduces reasoning time dramatically
Rewrote clarify.py to apply sensible defaults without an LLM call for short prompts
Fixed validation/runner.py: open IFC file with ifcopenshell.open() before passing to ifctester
Fixed validation/runner.py: replaced buggy Json reporter with direct spec status extraction
Fixed hk_code.ids: changed IsExternal dataType from IFCLABEL to IFCBOOLEAN
Fixed validate node: filters out empty/invalid IDS files before running validation
Increased request_timeout to 300s as safety margin
Added singleton caching and reset_llm() for LLM instance management

test_commands.verify_llm_works: uv run python -c "from agent.llm import get_llm; llm = get_llm(); r = llm.invoke('Say hi'); print(r.content)"
test_commands.run_pipeline_test: uv run python -c "from agent.graph import run_pipeline; r = run_pipeline('a 3x4m box house with one door and one window'); print('Status:', r.get('status'), 'IFC:', r.get('final_ifc_path'))"

project_status.full_pipeline: working (~2 min end-to-end, validation passes 3/3 IDS specs)
